page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.xlarge is a burstable virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng are categorized as weak, falling in the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth for large block writes is average at 26.23 GB/sec, but read-only bandwidth and decompression tasks, such as bzip3, fall into the weak to poor categories. Redis SET operations reach 4.94 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 4.16 GB/sec.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation and lack of local storage, this instance is designed for cost-sensitive, low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for light web serving, development environments, code repositories, or basic background tasks that experience occasional spikes in CPU utilization rather than sustained high-performance computing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance designed for budget-conscious workloads. Built on the KVM hypervisor, it features 4 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate weak overall performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the bottom 25%, though large-block memory write bandwidth remains average at 26.23 GB/sec. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for low-intensity applications. It is best suited for light web serving, development environments, and microservices that require occasional CPU bursting rather than sustained high-compute performance.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for cost-effective, low-intensity utility workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, optimized for budget-friendly, light workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly burstable instance offering balanced memory and compute resources for light, non-sustained development and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Burstable CPU allocation with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average large-block memory write bandwidth of 26.23 GB/sec
Suited for light web serving, microservices, and development environments
